segments.text: When Jesus had spoken these words, he went forth with his disciples over the brook Cedron, where was a garden, into the which he entered, and his disciples.
And Judas also, which betrayed him, knew the place: for Jesus ofttimes resorted thither with his disciples.
Judas then, having received a band of men and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees, cometh thither with lanterns and torches and weapons.
Jesus therefore, knowing all things that should come upon him, went forth,
and said unto them,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus of Nazareth.
segments.speaker: Unidentified Group

segments.text: And Judas also, which betrayed him, stood with them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As soon then as he had said unto them,
I am he,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: they went backward, and fell to the ground.
Then asked he them again,
Jesus of Nazareth.
segments.speaker: Unidentified Group

segments.text: Jesus answered,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: That the saying might be fulfilled, which he spake, Of them which thou gavest me have I lost none.
Then Simon Peter having a sword drew it, and smote the high priest's servant, and cut off his right ear. The servant's name was Malchus.
Then said Jesus unto Peter,
Then the band and the captain and officers of the Jews took Jesus, and bound him,
And led him away to Annas first; for he was father in law to Caiaphas, which was the high priest that same year.
Now Caiaphas was he, which gave counsel to the Jews, that it was expedient that one man should die for the people.
And Simon Peter followed Jesus, and so did another disciple: that disciple was known unto the high priest, and went in with Jesus into the palace of the high priest.
But Peter stood at the door without. Then went out that other disciple, which was known unto the high priest, and spake unto her that kept the door, and brought in Peter.
Then saith the damsel that kept the door unto Peter,
Art not thou also one of this man's disciples?
segments.speaker: Doorkeeper

segments.text: I am not.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: And the servants and officers stood there, who had made a fire of coals; for it was cold: and they warmed themselves: and Peter stood with them, and warmed himself.
The high priest then asked Jesus of his disciples, and of his doctrine.
Jesus answered him,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And when he had thus spoken, one of the officers which stood by struck Jesus with the palm of his hand,
Answerest thou the high priest so?
segments.speaker: Officer

segments.text: Jesus answered him,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now Annas had sent him bound unto Caiaphas the high priest.
And Simon Peter stood and warmed himself.
Art not thou also one of his disciples?
segments.speaker: Unidentified Group

segments.text: I am not.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: One of the servants of the high priest, being his kinsman whose ear Peter cut off, saith,
Did not I see thee in the garden with him?
segments.speaker: Servant of High Priest

segments.text: Peter then denied again: and immediately the cock crew.
Then led they Jesus from Caiaphas unto the hall of judgment: and it was early; and they themselves went not into the judgment hall, lest they should be defiled; but that they might eat the passover.
Pilate then went out unto them, and said,
What accusation bring ye against this man?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: If he were not a malefactor, we would not have delivered him up unto thee.
segments.speaker: Jewish Leaders

segments.text: Then said Pilate unto them,
Take ye him, and judge him according to your law.
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: It is not lawful for us to put any man to death:
segments.speaker: Jewish Leaders

segments.text: That the saying of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spake, signifying what death he should die.
Then Pilate entered into the judgment hall again, and called Jesus, and said unto him,
Art thou the King of the Jews?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: Jesus answered him,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Am I a Jew? Thine own nation and the chief priests have delivered thee unto me: what hast thou done?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: Jesus answered,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Art thou a king then?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: Jesus answered,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What is truth?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: And when he had said this, he went out again unto the Jews, and saith unto them,
I find in him no fault at all.
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: But ye have a custom, that I should release unto you one at the passover: will ye therefore that I release unto you the King of the Jews?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: Not this man, but Barabbas.
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: Now Barabbas was a robber.
